methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
ZeroAvia is a Series C-stage transportation company that has raised $150M+ to date. The relevant peer universe consists of seven private companies in the transportation sector, including Einride, Rivian, and REGENT Craft, with no direct public comparables available at a similar stage or valuation tier.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation tool here. Using total capital raised of $150M as a baseline proxy for post-Series B valuation, application of a 1.7x step-up multiplier—standard for progression from Series C to later rounds—yields an implied valuation of $255M. The peer-median approach could not be reliably applied; while five transportation peers exist broadly, the dataset contained fewer than three direct same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables needed to establish a statistically meaningful median. Relying on all-sector medians would introduce excessive noise given the concentrated nature of this subsector.

The triangulation analysis, supported by the single applicable method, indicates a valuation range of $217M (low) to $293M (high), with a median point of $255M. The width of this range reflects parametric sensitivity around the stage multiplier rather than cross-method divergence. Key uncertainties include the absence of near-peer transaction data, making the appropriateness of the 1.7x multiplier difficult to validate independently, and the reliance on total-raised as a valuation proxy, which may not fully capture interim dilution or market conditions specific to ZeroAvia's fundraising timing.
